title: Circuit Analysis: Induction Heads
relevance: Induction heads are the most well-studied attention circuit in mechanistic interpretability — discovered by Olsson et al. (2022) and central to Neel Nanda's 200 Concrete Open Problems. Detecting them programmatically is a foundational skill for circuit analysis, the core methodology of your LASR preparation and the SAIGE interview track.
hints: The induction score for a repeated sequence of length L=2k is: mean(attn[i, i-k+1]) for i in range(k, L). This is the average weight on the 'one-step-after-prev-occurrence' position in the second half.

For classify_head, check induction_score first — before previous_token_score. Induction heads in the first half have no signal, so their average previous-token-like attention across the whole sequence is lower than a true previous-token head's.
